title: OREGON TEACHER SCHOLARS PROGRAM GRANT - 211info
text: HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON TEACHER SCHOLARS PROGRAM GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding for students attending post secondary college who are culturally or linguistically diverse that are enrolled and pursuing their preliminary licensure for teaching, school counseling, school social work, and school psychology. Grant is limited, and based on available funding. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Be an Oregon resident for at least 12 months prior to college attendance. -Attend and be in good standing at an eligible Oregon four year-public and nonprofit college or university -Be enrolled and pursuing an approved preliminary licensure program in teaching, school counseling, school social work, and school psychology -Be within two years of completing a licensure program (or the equivalent number of remaining credits for part-time programs -Applicants are prioritized based on Student Aid Index (SAI) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 687-7400 ext 2 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Completed FAFSA LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State Of Oregon Services: Student Financial Aid Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - CHAFEE EDUCATION AND TRAINING VOUCHER PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - DECEASED OR DISABLED PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICER GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OFFICE OF STUDENT ACCESS AND COMPLETION EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON BARBER AND HAIRDRESSER GRANT PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON OPPORTUNITY GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROMISE GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON TRIBAL STUDENT GRANT MILITARY SERVICE - OREGON NATIONAL GUARD STATE TUITION ASSISTANCE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON STUDENT CHILD CARE GRANT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION
